system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,R)      Gauss upwind;
    div(R)          Gauss linear;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;

    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis

## Error Analysis

The error is:

```
Attempt to cast type zeroGradient to type nutWallFunction

From function To& Foam::refCast(From&) [...]
in file .../OpenFOAM-10/src/OpenFOAM/lnInclude/typeInfo.H at line 114.

...

#3  Foam::epsilonWallFunctionFvPatchScalarField::calculate(...)
...
#7  Foam::GeometricBoundaryField<double, Foam::fvPatchField, Foam::volMesh>::updateCoeffs()
...
#8  Foam::RASModels::kEpsilon<Foam::compressibleMomentumTransportModel>::correct()
```

This error occurs because the `nut` (turbulent viscosity) field has `type zeroGradient;` on wall patches, but the `kEpsilon` turbulence model with `epsilonWallFunction` requires `nut` to use `nutWallFunction` (a.k.a. `wallFunction`-based) boundary conditions on walls, not `zeroGradient`.

The `zeroGradient` type cannot be cast to `nutWallFunction`, which is what the `kEpsilon` model expects for `nut` on wall boundaries.

## Root Cause

In the `0/nut` file, the wall patches (`topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`) use `type zeroGradient;`. However, since the `kEpsilon` model is active and computes `epsilon` using `epsilonWallFunction`, the `nut` field must use a proper wall-function boundary condition — specifically `nutWallFunction` (which is the standard wall-type BC for `nut` in `buoyantFoam` with `kEpsilon`).

## Proposed Solution

### Fix `0/nut` — Change wall patch boundary conditions from `zeroGradient` to `nutWallFunction`

In `0/nut`, change the boundary conditions for `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, and `rightWall` from:

```
topWall
{
    type            zeroGradient;
}

bottomWall
{
    type            zeroGradient;
}

leftWall
{
    type            zeroGradient;
}

rightWall
{
    type            zeroGradient;
}
```

To:

```
topWall
{
    type            nutWallFunction;
}

bottomWall
{
    type            nutWallFunction;
}

leftWall
{
    type            nutWallFunction;
}

rightWall
{
    type            nutWallFunction;
}
```

Keep `frontAndBack` as `type empty;` since that patch is already correct.

The `nutWallFunction` is the standard wall-type boundary condition used by the `kEpsilon` turbulence model for the `nut` field. This is consistent with how the Bernard Cells tutorial case handles it — wall patches get `nutWallFunction` (not `zeroGradient`) so that the turbulent viscosity properly transitions to zero at the wall while maintaining the correct gradient behavior required by the turbulence model.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': '0/nut', 'changes': 'Change topWall boundary type from zeroGradient to nutWallFunction; Change bottomWall boundary type from zeroGradient to nutWallFunction; Change leftWall boundary type from zeroGradient to nutWallFunction; Change rightWall boundary type from zeroGradient to nutWallFunction'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t=1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 301 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
